body.title: /audit-ticket: flag hold-status without named dependency
body.result: Check 18 added to /audit-ticket: hold-status requires T-xxx dependency or Akien: action; AMEND if absent; output format + hard rules updated; synced to ~/.claude/skills/
body.intention: I intend that no hold-status ticket passes /audit-ticket without explicitly naming what it is blocked on.
body.description:
**Problem:** Tickets are occasionally filed with status=hold for vague reasons (imagined external impact, hypothetical future components) rather than real dependencies. These drift stale and confuse the queue.

**Proposed shape:** Add a hold-validation check to /audit-ticket: if the drafted ticket has status=hold, the description must contain either a named ticket ID (T-xxx) being blocked on, or an explicit 'Akien:' action required. If neither present, return AMEND with the specific missing item.

**Affected files:** skills/audit-ticket.md

**Design rules:** Holds without named dependencies are not permitted (CC memory 2026-06-06; feedback: CC has history of spurious holds).

**Scope boundary:** One check added to audit-ticket skill. No code changes.

**Completion criteria:** skills/audit-ticket.md contains the hold-dependency check; a draft ticket with status=hold and no named dependency returns AMEND when /audit-ticket runs.
